info.title: DeviceUpdateClient
info.description: Device Update for IoT Hub is an Azure service that enables customers to publish updates for their IoT devices to the cloud, and then deploy that update to their devices (approve updates to groups of devices managed and provisioned in IoT Hub). It leverages the proven security and reliability of the Windows Update platform, optimized for IoT devices. It works globally and knows when and how to update devices, enabling customers to focus on their business goals and let Device Update for IoT Hub handle the updates.

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description: The Device Update for IoT Hub account endpoint (hostname only, no protocol).

get.description:
Gets a list of all device classes (sets of devices compatible with the same
updates based on the model Id and compat properties reported in the Device
Update PnP interface in IoT Hub) for all devices connected to Device Update for
IoT Hub.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Restricts the set of device classes returned. You can filter on friendly name.

get.description: Gets the properties of a device class.

parameters.name: deviceClassPatch
parameters.description:
The device class json merge patch body. Currently only supports patching
friendlyName.

delete.description:
Deletes a device class. Device classes are created automatically when Device
Update-enabled devices are connected to the hub but are not automatically
cleaned up since they are referenced by DeviceClassSubgroups. If the user has
deleted all DeviceClassSubgroups for a device class they can also delete the
device class to remove the records from the system and to stop checking the
compatibility of this device class with new updates. If a device is ever
reconnected for this device class it will be re-created.

204.description: There is no content to send for this request, but the headers may be useful.

get.description: Gets a list of installable updates for a device class.
Get list of device health

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Restricts the set of devices for which device health is returned. You can
filter on status, device id and module id.

get.description: Get all device diagnostics log collections
Get the device diagnostics log collection

put.description: Start the device diagnostics log collection on specified devices.

parameters.name: logCollection
parameters.description: The log collection properties.

201.description: The request has succeeded and a new resource has been created as a result.

get.description: Get log collection with detailed status
Gets a list of devices connected to Device Update for IoT Hub.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Restricts the set of devices returned. You can filter on GroupId,
DeviceClassId, or GroupId and DeploymentStatus. Use DeploymentStatus eq null to
query for devices with no deployment status (that have never been deployed to).

get.description:
Gets the device properties and latest deployment status for a device connected
to Device Update for IoT Hub.

Gets the device module properties and latest deployment status for a device
module connected to Device Update for IoT Hub.

post.description:
Import existing devices from IoT Hub. This is a long-running-operation; use
Operation-Location response header value to check for operation status.

parameters.name: importType
parameters.description: The types of devices to import.

202.description: The request has been accepted for processing, but processing has not yet completed.

Operation-Location.description: The location for monitoring the operation state.

get.description:
Gets a list of all device groups.  The $default group will always be returned
first.

parameters.name: orderby
parameters.description:
Orders the set of groups returned. You can order by groupId, deviceCount,
createdDate, subgroupsWithNewUpdatesAvailableCount,
subgroupsWithUpdatesInProgressCount, or subgroupsOnLatestUpdateCount.

get.description: Gets the device group properties.

delete.description:
Deletes a device group. This group is automatically created when a Device
Update-enabled device is connected to the hub and reports its properties.
Groups, subgroups, and deployments are not automatically cleaned up but are
retained for history purposes. Users can call this method to delete a group if
they do not need to retain any of the history of the group and no longer need
it. If a device is ever connected again for this group after the group was
deleted it will be automatically re-created but there will be no history.

204.description: There is no content to send for this request, but the headers may be useful.

get.description:
Get the best available updates for a device group and a count of how many
devices need each update.
Gets a list of deployments for a device group.

parameters.name: orderby
parameters.description: Orders the set of deployments returned. You can order by start date.

get.description: Gets the deployment properties.

parameters.name: deployment
parameters.description: The deployment properties.

204.description: There is no content to send for this request, but the headers may be useful.

get.description:
Gets the status of a deployment including a breakdown of how many devices in
the deployment are in progress, completed, or failed.

Get the device class subgroups for the group. A device class subgroup is the
set of devices within the group that share the same device class. All devices
within the same device class are compatible with the same updates.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Restricts the set of device class subgroups returned. You can filter on compat
properties by name and value. (i.e. filter=compatProperties/propertyName1 eq
'value1' and compatProperties/propertyName2 eq 'value2')

get.description:
Gets device class subgroup details. A device class subgroup is the set of
devices within the group that share the same device class. All devices within
the same device class are compatible with the same updates.

delete.description:
Deletes a device class subgroup. This subgroup is automatically created when a
Device Update-enabled device is connected to the hub and reports its
properties. Groups, subgroups, and deployments are not automatically cleaned up
but are retained for history purposes. Users can call this method to delete a
subgroup if they do not need to retain any of the history of the subgroup and
no longer need it. If a device is ever connected again for this subgroup after
the subgroup was deleted it will be automatically re-created but there will be
no history.

204.description: There is no content to send for this request, but the headers may be useful.

get.description:
Get the best available update for a device class subgroup and a count of how
many devices need this update.
Gets a list of deployments for a device class subgroup.

parameters.name: orderby
parameters.description: Orders the set of deployments returned. You can order by start date.

get.description: Gets the deployment properties.

delete.description: Deletes a device class subgroup deployment.

204.description: There is no content to send for this request, but the headers may be useful.

post.description: Retries a deployment with failed devices.

get.description:
Gets a list of devices in a deployment along with their state. Useful for
getting a list of failed devices.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Restricts the set of deployment device states returned. You can filter on
deviceId and moduleId and/or deviceState.

get.description:
Gets the status of a deployment including a breakdown of how many devices in
the deployment are in progress, completed, or failed.

Get device class subgroup update compliance information such as how many
devices are on their latest update, how many need new updates, and how many are
in progress on receiving a new update.

Get device group update compliance information such as how many devices are on
their latest update, how many need new updates, and how many are in progress on
receiving a new update.

Get a list of all device import operations. Completed operations are kept for 7
days before auto-deleted.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Restricts the set of operations returned. Only one specific filter is
supported: "status eq 'NotStarted' or status eq 'Running'"

parameters.name: top
parameters.description:
Specifies a non-negative integer n that limits the number of items returned
from a collection. The service returns the number of available items up to but
not greater than the specified value n.

get.description: Retrieve operation status.

Retry-After.description: Number of seconds to wait before checking the operation status again.

get.description:
Gets the breakdown of how many devices are on their latest update, have new
updates available, or are in progress receiving new updates.
Get a list of all updates that have been imported to Device Update for IoT Hub.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Request updates matching a free-text search expression.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Optional to filter updates by isDeployable property.

get.description:
Get a list of all import update operations. Completed operations are kept for 7
days before auto-deleted. Delete operations are not returned by this API
version.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Optional to filter operations by status property. Only one specific filter is
supported: "status eq 'NotStarted' or status eq 'Running'"

parameters.name: top
parameters.description:
Specifies a non-negative integer n that limits the number of items returned
from a collection. The service returns the number of available items up to but
not greater than the specified value n.

get.description: Retrieve operation status.

Retry-After.description: Number of seconds to wait before checking the operation status again.

get.description:
Get a list of all update providers that have been imported to Device Update for
IoT Hub.
Get a list of all update names that match the specified provider.
Get a list of all update versions that match the specified provider and name.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Optional to filter updates by isDeployable property.

get.description: Get a specific update version.

delete.description:
Delete a specific update version. This is a long-running-operation; use
Operation-Location response header value to check for operation status.

202.description: The request has been accepted for processing, but processing has not yet completed.

Operation-Location.description: The location for monitoring the operation state.

get.description: Get a list of all update file identifiers for the specified version.
Get a specific update file from the version.

post.description:
Import new update version. This is a long-running-operation; use
Operation-Location response header value to check for operation status.

parameters.name: updateToImport
parameters.description:
The update to be imported (see schema
https://json.schemastore.org/azure-deviceupdate-import-manifest-5.0.json for
details).

202.description: The request has been accepted for processing, but processing has not yet completed.

Operation-Location.description: The location for monitoring the operation state.

failure.description: Failure conditions to initiate rollback policy.

CloudInitiatedRollbackPolicyFailure.description: Failure conditions to initiate rollback policy

Compatibility.description: Key-value pairs representing update compatibility information.

ContractModel.description: The Device Update agent contract model.

id.description:
The Device Update agent contract model Id of the device class. This is also
used to calculate the device class Id.

name.description:
The Device Update agent contract model name of the device class. Intended to be
a more readable form of the contract model Id.

deploymentId.description:
The caller-provided deployment identifier. This cannot be longer than 73
characters, must be all lower-case, and cannot contain '&', '^', '[', ']', '{',
'}', '|', '<', '>', forward slash, backslash, or double quote. The Updates view
in the Azure Portal IoT Hub resource generates a GUID for deploymentId when you
create a deployment.

update.description: Update information for the update in the deployment.

groupId.description: The group identity for the devices the deployment is intended to update.

deviceClassSubgroups.description:
The device class subgroups the deployment is compatible with and subgroup
deployments have been created for. This is not provided by the caller during
CreateOrUpdateDeployment but is automatically determined by Device Update

isCanceled.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the deployment was canceled.

isRetried.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the deployment has been retried.

rollbackPolicy.description: The rollback policy for the deployment.

isCloudInitiatedRollback.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the deployment is a rollback deployment.

retryCount.description: The number of times this deployment has been retried on this device.

movedOnToNewDeployment.description:
Boolean flag indicating whether this device is in a newer deployment and can no
longer retry this deployment.

DeploymentDeviceStatesList.description: The list of deployment device states.

value.description: The DeploymentDeviceState items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

values.name: Active
values.description: The deployment can be sent to devices targeted in the deployment.

values.name: ActiveWithSubgroupFailures
values.description:
The deployment can be sent to some devices targeted in the deployment but at
least 1 subgroup is in a failed state.

values.name: Failed
values.description:
The deployment will not be sent to any devices.  Consult error for more details
about what failed.

values.name: Inactive
values.description:
A newer deployment for this group has been created and no devices in the group
will receive this deployment.

values.name: Canceled
values.description: The deployment has been canceled and no devices will receive it.

error.description:
The error details of the Failed state.  This is not present if the deployment
state is not Failed.

subgroupStatus.description: The collection of device class subgroup status objects

value.description: The Deployment items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

lastAttemptedUpdate.description: The update that device last attempted to install.

deploymentStatus.description: State of the device in its last deployment.

onLatestUpdate.description:
Boolean flag indicating whether the latest update (the best compatible update
for the device's device class and group) is installed on the device

lastDeploymentId.description: The deployment identifier for the last deployment to the device

deviceClassId.description:
The device class identifier. This is generated from the model Id and the compat
properties reported by the device update agent in the Device Update PnP
interface in IoT Hub. It is a hex-encoded SHA1 hash.

friendlyName.description:
The device class friendly name. This can be updated by callers after the device
class has been automatically created.

deviceClassProperties.description: The device class properties that are used to calculate the device class Id

bestCompatibleUpdate.description: Update that is the highest version compatible with this device class.

DeviceClassFilter.description: Device class filter. Filters device classes based on device class friendly name

friendlyName.description: The friendly name of the device class to use in the filter

DeviceClassProperties.description: The device class properties that are used to calculate the device class Id

contractModel.description: The Device Update agent contract model.

compatProperties.description:
The compat properties of the device class. This object can be thought of as a
set of key-value pairs where the key is the name of the compatibility property
and the value is the value of the compatibility property. There will always be
at least 1 compat property

DeviceClassSubgroup.description:
Device class subgroup details. A device class subgroup is a subset of devices
in a group that share the same device class id.

deviceClassId.description:
Device class subgroup identity. This is generated from the model Id and the
compat properties reported by the device update agent in the Device Update PnP
interface in IoT Hub. It is a hex-encoded SHA1 hash.

createdDateTime.description: Date and time when the device class subgroup was created.

deviceCount.description: The number of devices in the device class subgroup.

deploymentId.description: The active deployment Id for the device class subgroup.

values.name: Active
values.description: The subgroup deployment can be sent to devices targeted in the deployment.

values.name: Failed
values.description: The subgroup deployment failed and will not be sent to any devices.

values.name: Inactive
values.description:
A newer deployment for this subgroup has been created and no devices in the
subgroup will receive this deployment.

values.name: Canceled
values.description: The subgroup deployment has been canceled and no devices will receive it.

DeviceClassSubgroupDeploymentStatus.description: Device class subgroup deployment status metadata.

deploymentState.description: The state of the subgroup deployment.

error.description:
The error details of the Failed state.  This is not present if the deployment
state is not Failed.

totalDevices.description: The total number of devices in the deployment.

devicesInProgressCount.description: The number of devices that are currently in deployment.

devicesCompletedFailedCount.description: The number of devices that have completed deployment with a failure.

devicesCompletedSucceededCount.description: The number of devices which have successfully completed deployment.

devicesCanceledCount.description: The number of devices which have had their deployment canceled.

DeviceClassSubgroupFilter.description:
Device class subgroups filter. Filters device class subgroups based on device
class compat property names and values

compatPropertyName.description:
The name of the compat property to use in the filter. E.g.
compatProperties/manufacturer

compatPropertyValue.description: The value the compat property to use in the filter. E.g. Contoso

DeviceClassSubgroupUpdatableDevices.description:
Device class subgroup, update information, and the number of devices for which
the update is applicable.

deviceClassId.description: The device class subgroup's device class Id

deviceCount.description: Total number of devices for which the update is applicable.

DeviceClassSubgroupUpdatableDevicesList.description: The list of updatable devices for a device class subgroup.

value.description: The DeviceClassSubgroupUpdatableDevices items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

DeviceClassSubgroupsList.description: The list of device class subgroups within a group.

value.description: The DeviceClassSubgroup items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

value.description: The DeviceClass items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

values.name: Succeeded
values.description: Deployment has completed with success.

values.name: InProgress
values.description: Deployment is in progress.

values.name: Canceled
values.description: Deployment was canceled.

values.name: Failed
values.description: Deployment has completed with failure.

deploymentStatus.description: State of the device in its last deployment.

healthChecks.description: Array of health checks and their results

DeviceHealthList.description: Array of Device Health, with server paging support.

value.description: The DeviceHealth items on this page

values.name: healthy
values.description: Agent is healthy

values.name: unhealthy
values.description: Agent is in an unhealthy state

traceId.description:
Operation correlation identity that can used by Microsoft Support for
troubleshooting.

lastActionDateTime.description: Date and time in UTC when the operation status was last updated.

createdDateTime.description: Date and time in UTC when the operation was created.

DeviceOperationsList.description: The list of device operations with server paging support.

value.description: The DeviceOperation items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

value.description: The Device items on this page

message.description: A human-readable representation of the error.

details.description: An array of errors that led to the reported error.

innererror.description:
An object containing more specific information than the current object about
the error.

occurredDateTime.description: Date and time in UTC when the error occurred.

filename.description: Update file name as specified inside import manifest.

url.description:
Azure Blob location from which the update file can be downloaded by Device
Update for IoT Hub. This is typically a read-only SAS-protected blob URL with
an expiration set to at least 4 hours.

groupId.description:
Group identity. This is created from the value of the ADUGroup tag in the Iot
Hub's device/module twin or $default for devices with no tag.

createdDateTime.description: Date and time when the update was created.

deviceCount.description: The number of devices in the group.

subgroupsWithNewUpdatesAvailableCount.description: The count of subgroups with new updates available.

subgroupsWithUpdatesInProgressCount.description: The count of subgroups with updates in progress.

subgroupsWithOnLatestUpdateCount.description: The count of subgroups with devices on the latest update.

deployments.description: The active deployment Ids for the group

subgroupsWithNewUpdatesAvailableCount.description: The number of subgroups with new updates available

subgroupsWithUpdatesInProgressCount.description: The number of subgroups with updates in progress

subgroupsOnLatestUpdateCount.description: The number of subgroups with devices on the latest update

values.name: IoTHubTag
values.description: IoT Hub tag based group, all devices in the group share an ADUGroup tag value.

values.name: DefaultNoTag
values.description: Default group for untagged devices.

value.description: The Group items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

values.name: success
values.description: Health check succeeded

values.name: userError
values.description: Health check failed due to user error

ImportManifestMetadata.description:
Metadata describing the import manifest, a document which describes the files
and other metadata about an update version.

url.description:
Azure Blob location from which the import manifest can be downloaded by Device
Update for IoT Hub. This is typically a read-only SAS-protected blob URL with
an expiration set to at least 4 hours.

sizeInBytes.description: File size in number of bytes.

hashes.description:
A JSON object containing the hash(es) of the file. At least SHA256 hash is
required. This object can be thought of as a set of key-value pairs where the
key is the hash algorithm, and the value is the hash of the file calculated
using that algorithm.

values.name: Devices
values.description: Import only devices but not modules.

values.name: Modules
values.description: Import only modules but not devices.

values.name: All
values.description: Import both devices and modules.

ImportUpdateInput.description: Update input data list (array of update items to import).

importManifest.description: Import manifest metadata like source URL, file size/hashes, etc.

files.description: One or more update file properties like filename and source URL.

InnerError.description:
An object containing more specific information than the current object about
the error.

code.description: A more specific error code than what was provided by the containing error.

message.description: A human-readable representation of the error.

errorDetail.description: The internal error or exception message.

innerError.description:
An object containing more specific information than the current object about
the error.

InstallResult.description: The install result of an update and any step results under it.

resultDetails.description: A string containing further details about the install result

deviceList.description: Array of Device Update agent ids

createdDateTime.description: The timestamp when the operation was created.

lastActionDateTime.description: A timestamp for when the current state was entered.

LogCollectionDetailedStatusList.description: The list of log collections with detailed status, with server paging support.

value.description: The LogCollectionOperationDetailedStatus items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

LogCollectionList.description: The list of log collections with server paging support.

value.description: The LogCollection items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

createdDateTime.description: The timestamp when the operation was created.

lastActionDateTime.description: A timestamp for when the current state was entered.

deviceStatus.description: Status of the devices in the operation

values.name: Running
values.description: Running

values.name: NotStarted
values.description: NotStarted

values.name: NotStarted
values.description: Background operation created but not started yet.

values.name: Running
values.description: Background operation is currently running.

values.name: Succeeded
values.description: Background operation finished with success.

values.name: Failed
values.description: Background operation finished with failure.

PatchBody.description: Device Class JSON Merge Patch request body

friendlyName.description:
The device class friendly name. Friendly name can be 1-100 characters,
alphanumeric, dot, and dash.

values.name: Inline
values.description: Step type that performs code execution.

values.name: Reference
values.description: Step type that installs another update.

handler.description:
Identity of handler that will execute this step. Required if step type is
inline.

handlerProperties.description: Parameters to be passed to handler during execution.

files.description:
Collection of file names to be passed to handler during execution. Required if
step type is inline.

updateId.description: Referenced child update identity.  Required if step type is reference.

StepResult.description: The step result under an update.

update.description: The update that this step installs if it is of reference type.

resultDetails.description: A string containing further details about the install result

values.name: Inline
values.description: Step type that performs code execution.

values.name: Reference
values.description: Step type that installs another update.

StringsList.description: The list of strings with server paging support.

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items.

friendlyName.description: Friendly update name specified by importer.

isDeployable.description: Whether the update can be deployed to a device on its own.

updateType.description: Update type. Deprecated in latest import manifest schema.

installedCriteria.description:
String interpreted by Device Update client to determine if the update is
installed on the device. Deprecated in latest import manifest schema.

referencedBy.description: List of update identities that reference this update.

scanResult.description: Update aggregate scan result (calculated from payload file scan results).

manifestVersion.description: Schema version of manifest used to import the update.

importedDateTime.description: Date and time in UTC when the update was imported.

createdDateTime.description: Date and time in UTC when the update was created.

onLatestUpdateDeviceCount.description: Number of devices on the latest update.

newUpdatesAvailableDeviceCount.description: Number of devices with a newer update available.

updatesInProgressDeviceCount.description: Number of devices with update in-progress.

fileId.description: File identity, generated by server at import time.

relatedFiles.description:
Optional related files metadata used together DownloadHandler metadata to
download payload file.

downloadHandler.description: Optional download handler for utilizing related files to download payload file.

sizeInBytes.description: File size in number of bytes.

hashes.description: Mapping of hashing algorithm to base64 encoded hash values.

properties.description: Optional file properties (not consumed by service but pass-through to device).

UpdateFileDownloadHandler.description: Download handler for utilizing related files to download payload file.

The update being imported or deleted. For import, this property will only be
populated after import manifest is processed successfully.

resourceLocation.description: Location of the imported update when operation is successful.

traceId.description:
Operation correlation identity that can used by Microsoft Support for

Azure.Core.Foundations.ApiVersionParamet.description: The API version to use for this operation.

Defines the If-None-Match condition. The operation will be performed only if
the ETag on the server does not match this value.
